title: Day 1 — Alphabet & Pronunciation
goal: 🎯 Today you learn to read German aloud, spell your name, and say your first 16 words.
introHtml:
Day 1 · The Alphabet, Pronunciation & First Words

🎯 Today you learn to read German aloud, spell your name, and say your first 16 words.

sections.title: 📖 Grammar & pronunciation lesson
sections.html:
📖 Grammar & pronunciation lesson

1. The German alphabet (das Alphabet)

Same 26 letters as English, plus ä ö ü ß. The letter names matter — Germans spell names on the phone constantly.

A "ah"B "bay"C "tsay"D "day"
E "ay"F "eff"G "gay"H "hah"
I "ee"J "yot"K "kah"L "ell"
M "emm"N "enn"O "oh"P "pay"
Q "koo"R "err"S "ess"T "tay"
U "oo"V "fow"W "vay"X "iks"
Y "üpsilon"Z "tsett"ß "ess-tsett"ä/ö/ü = a/o/u + Umlaut

2. Three golden rules

  • German is phonetic. Words sound how they're written, once you know the rules (see the crash course on the Start page — read it now if you skipped it!).
  • Stress the first syllable of most German words: AR-beiten, MOR-gen.
  • All nouns get a capital letter: der Morgen, die Nacht.
💡 Practice pairs aloud: Wein ("vine" = wine) vs Wien ("veen" = Vienna). ei = "eye", ie = "ee".

sections.title: 📚 Vocabulary (16 words)
sections.html:
📚 Vocabulary (16 words)
GermanEnglish
Hallohello
Guten Morgengood morning
Guten Taggood day / hello (daytime)
Guten Abendgood evening
Gute Nachtgood night (before sleeping)
Tschüssbye (informal)
Auf Wiedersehengoodbye (formal)
jayes
neinno
danke (schön)thank you (very much)
bitteplease / you're welcome
Entschuldigungexcuse me / sorry
Wie bitte?pardon? / say that again?
undand
oderor
Alles klarall right / got it
💡 bitte is the Swiss Army knife of German: "please", "you're welcome", "here you go", and "pardon?" (Wie bitte?).

sections.title: 💬 Example sentences
sections.html:
💬 Example sentences
  1. Guten Morgen! Good morning!
  2. Hallo, alles klar? Hi, everything OK?
  3. Danke schön! — Bitte! Thanks a lot! — You're welcome!
  4. Entschuldigung, wie bitte? Sorry, could you say that again?
  5. Ja oder nein? Yes or no?
  6. Tschüss, gute Nacht! Bye, good night!

sections.title: 🗣 Dialogue: Im Café (at the café)
sections.html:
🗣 Dialogue: Im Café (at the café)

Anna: Guten Tag! (Good day!)

Ben: Guten Tag! Kaffee? (Good day! Coffee?)

Anna: Ja, bitte. (Yes, please.)

Ben: Bitte schön. (Here you go.)

Anna: Danke schön! (Thank you very much!)

Ben: Bitte. Auf Wiedersehen! (You're welcome. Goodbye!)

Anna: Tschüss! (Bye!)

Read it aloud 3 times. Then swap roles and read it again.

sections.title: 🎤 Speaking practice
sections.html:
🎤 Speaking practice
  1. Spell your full name aloud using the German letter names. Do it 3 times, faster each time.
  2. Say each greeting matched to the right time of day: it's 8:00 → Guten Morgen!; 14:00 → ?; 20:00 → ?; going to bed → ?
  3. Read these aloud using the rules: Wasser, Vater, ja, zehn, Sie, nein, wie, neun, Haus, Schule, Straße, ich, Buch, schön, fünf.
  4. Record yourself on your phone saying all 16 vocabulary words. Listen back — does your W sound like a V?

sections.title: 🎧 Listening practice
sections.html:
🎧 Listening practice
  • Search YouTube for "German alphabet song" — listen twice, sing along once.
  • Start Nicos Weg A1, Episode 1 ("Hallo!") on the DW Learn German site or YouTube. Watch once with subtitles, once without.
  • Type today's 16 words into Google Translate or DeepL and tap 🔊 to hear each one. Repeat aloud after each.

sections.title: 📰 Reading practice
sections.html:
📰 Reading practice

Read this aloud, then answer the questions:

Guten Morgen! Kaffee oder Tee? — Kaffee, bitte. — Alles klar. Bitte schön! — Danke! — Bitte!

  1. What time of day is it (roughly)?
  2. What does the person order?
Answer key

1. Morning (Guten Morgen). 2. Coffee (Kaffee, bitte).

sections.title: ✍️ Writing task
sections.html:
✍️ Writing task
  1. Copy all 16 vocabulary words by hand, once each. Handwriting builds memory.
  2. Write a 4-line mini dialogue: greeting → offer (Kaffee? / Tee?) → answer with ja/nein + bitte/danke → goodbye.

sections.title: ❓ Mini quiz
sections.html:
❓ Mini quiz
  1. How do you say goodbye formally?
  2. Wein is pronounced: a) "veen" b) "vine" c) "wine"
  3. Which greeting fits at 21:00? a) Guten Morgen b) Guten Abend c) Gute Nacht
  4. Someone says Danke! — what do you reply?
  5. True or false: German nouns are always capitalized.
Answer key
  1. Auf Wiedersehen
  2. b — W sounds like V, ei sounds like "eye"
  3. b (Gute Nacht is only for bedtime)
  4. Bitte!
  5. True

sections.title: 🃏 Flashcards (tap to flip)
sections.html:
🃏 Flashcards (tap to flip)

Say the answer out loud, then tap to check.

helloHallo
good morningGuten Morgen
goodbye (formal)Auf Wiedersehen
bye (informal)Tschüss
thank youdanke
please / you're welcomebitte
excuse me / sorryEntschuldigung
pardon?Wie bitte?

sections.title: 📌 Homework
sections.html:
📌 Homework
  1. Put sticky notes with Hallo / Tschüss on your front door — say them every time you pass.
  2. Spell 3 more names aloud (a friend, your city, your street).
  3. Re-read the pronunciation crash course on the Start page.

sections.title: 🔄 Revision
sections.html:
🔄 Revision

First day — nothing to revise yet! Instead, read the "Top mistakes" list on the Start page so you avoid them from Day 2 on.
